text:
Market today: Cautious optimism. Bessent's 3% GDP talk gives bulls something to chew on, but tech/chips are still digesting macro uncertainty. Your names move on growth confidence and rate expectations — GDP optimism helps, but doesn't fix broken charts.

Your holdings: AVGO is your biggest problem — down 12.89%, largest position, on margin. That's a real dollar hole. PLTR is down 10.31% with nothing in the note to explain the slide — no catalyst to hold against. CRWD is down 10.29% too; three names bleeding double digits simultaneously on margin is stress you need to actively manage, not ignore. SPCX is a brand-new IPO with almost no trading history — highly volatile, tiny stop cushion ($6), and margin makes any gap-down dangerous.

Watch: 1) AVGO — does it stabilize or keep sliding toward that $336 stop? 2) The new Congress energy-cost bill for AI data centers — directly hits your chip and cloud names.

Bottom line: Three double-digit losers on margin demand a hard look at position sizing before the market decides for you.
